channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: 'It bothers me that Vinicius' celebration is being used against him'
item.description: Real Madrid goalkeeper Thibaut Courtois says Vinicius Jr's celebration against Benfica in last week's Champions League tie does not "justify a suspected act of racism".

item.title: 'It would be catastrophic' - are Spurs too big to go down?
item.description: Tottenham's heavy north London derby defeat against Arsenal on Sunday just confirmed what everyone already knew - Igor Tudor's team are in serious trouble. BBC Sport assesses their precarious situation, what relegation might bring and asks if they are too big to go down?

item.title: Why Champions League return is crucial to Man Utd
item.description: Up to fourth in the Premier League after Monday's win at Everton, Champions League qualification would be a big boost to Manchester United's finances after two years outside Europe's elite club competition.

item.title: I want to show everyone what I can do for Liverpool - Ngumoha
item.description: Rio Ngumoha on life at Liverpool, his mindset when he comes off the bench and his discussions with manager Arne Slot.

item.title: PSG's Hakimi to face trial after rape allegation
item.description: Paris St-Germain defender Achraf Hakimi says he is set to face trial after an allegation of rape was made against him.

item.title: Portugal want England U18 forward Mane
item.description: Wolves forward Mateus Mane is at the centre of a tug of war between England and Portugal.

item.title: New stadium curse - are Everton showing how clubs struggle after move?
item.description: As Everton's home form continues to struggle, BBC Sport takes a closer look at the curse of the new stadium in the Premier League era.

item.title: Who has made Troy's Premier League team of the week?
item.description: After every round of Premier League matches this season, Troy Deeney gives us his team of the week. Do you agree with his choices?

item.title: Leggings, gloves, snoods - football's fashion trends
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the number of fashion trends that have taken place in football.

item.title: When is Champions League last-16 draw and how does it work?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at when the Champions League knockout phase draw takes place and who Premier League teams could face.

item.title: Quiz - name every Englishman to score a Champions League hat-trick
item.description: In Newcastle's match with Qarabag, a certain winger became the 11th Englishmen to score a Champions League treble.

item.title: Snoop Dogg and Swansea - the story behind football's unlikeliest link-up
item.description: As Snoop Dogg attends his first Swansea City game, BBC Sport Wales looks at how the sport-loving American rapper became an investor in the Welsh club.

item.title: Super-sub Sesko earns Man Utd win at Everton
item.description: Watch Premier League highlights as Manchester United earn a narrow win over Everton thanks to a goal from substitute Benjamin Sesko.

item.title: Will EFL Cup final affect Premier League title race?
item.description: Mark Chapman, Theo Walcott and Chris Sutton discuss the potential impact of the Carabao Cup final on the Premier League title race.

item.title: Snoop Dogg meets the Swans
item.description: Legendary rapper Snoop Dogg paid a visit to the Swansea City training ground the day before watching the club for the first time since becoming an investor.

item.title: Carrick praises 'ruthless' goalscorer Sesko
item.description: Manchester United manager Michael Carrick praises the impact of striker Benjamin Sesko, who came off the bench to score in a 1-0 win at Everton.

item.title: Has Arteta solved the Gyokeres problem?
item.description: Umir Irfan analyses how Mikel Arteta got the best out of Viktor Gyokeres in the win over Tottenham and how Arsenal have not always played to the striker's strengths this season.

item.title: Could seven Premier League clubs qualify for the Champions League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how as many as seven Premier League clubs could be in next season's Champions League.

item.title: Barcelona sign 16-year-old Norwich prospect Tavares
item.description: Spanish giants Barcelona have signed 16-year-old Norwich City academy prospect Ajay Tavares, who has played for England age-group teams.

item.title: Weir 'more determined than ever' to make World Cup
item.description: Caroline Weir is "more determined that ever" to reach a World Cup after being installed as Melissa Andreatta's captain.

item.title: Man Utd consider McTominay return - Tuesday's gossip
item.description: Manchester United are considering bringing Scott McTominay back to Old Trafford, Real Madrid are keeping an eye on Micky van de Ven's contract situation, and Brighton are hoping to complete a deal for Said El Mala.

item.title: Fifa 'closely monitoring' Mexico cartel violence before World Cup
item.description: BBC Sport explores the unfolding security situation in Mexico, as Fifa says it is "closely monitoring" developments before this summer's World Cup.

item.title: Chelsea to host Spurs - full Women's FA Cup quarter-final draw
item.description: Chelsea host Tottenham and WSL 2 leaders Charlton Athletic face Liverpool in the Women's FA Cup quarter-finals.

item.title: Advocaat to miss Scotland friendly and World Cup history
item.description: Curacao will have a new head coach in charge for their World Cup warm-up friendly against Scotland on 30 May after Dick Advocaat stands down for personal reasons.

item.title: Police investigate 'abhorrent' racist abuse of players
item.description: Police confirm they are investigating after four Premier League players were targeted by online racial abuse at the weekend.

item.title: Sesko and Lammens allow Man Utd to dream of Champions League
item.description: Striker Benjamin Sesko and goalkeeper Senne Lammens are the key figures as Manchester United gain a hugely valuable Premier League win at Everton.

item.title: Unlicensed betting firms face ban on sponsoring British sports teams
item.description: Gambling firms not licensed in the UK could be banned from sponsoring British sports teams - including Premier League clubs - as part of a government crackdown.

item.title: Kings Lynn investigated over postponement for waterlogged pitch
item.description: The National League is conducting an inquiry into events before the recent South Shields fixture.

item.title: How league-worst late-goal record continues to cost Leeds
item.description: After conceding another late goal on Saturday, we look at the stats that show Leeds would be ninth in the Premier League if games ended after 85 minutes.

item.title: Everton worthy of more than a defeat - Moyes
item.description: Everton manager David Moyes says his side were "worth more than a defeat" after losing 1-0 to Manchester United in the Premier League.

item.title: Saka and Timber partnership among 'best in Europe'
item.description: Match of the Day pundit Danny Murphy looks at how the combination of Bukayo Saka and Jurrien Timber down the right side tormented Tottenham's defence in Arsenal's 4-1 win over their north London rivals.

item.title: Spurs' league position is a 'big concern' - Rooney
item.description: Wayne Rooney discusses the possibility of Tottenham Hotspur getting relegated this season.

item.title: Referee disallowed Spurs goal for 'soft' push - Given
item.description: Match of the Day pundits Shay Given and Danny Murphy believe Tottenham "have a reason to be disappointed" with Randal Kolo Muani's second 'goal' being ruled out for a foul on Arsenal defender Gabriel.

item.title: Gyokeres performance 'incredible' - Arteta
item.description: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta is full of praise for Viktor Gyokeres' "outstanding" performance in the Gunners 4-1 win over Tottenham. The Swedish striker bagged a brace to help his side go five points clear at the top of the Premier League.

item.title: Fifa wants injured players to stay off for one minute
item.description: Players who receive treatment for an injury could be forced to stay off the field for one minute under proposals from Fifa.

item.title: Benfica's Prestianni gets provisional one-match ban after Vinicius incident
item.description: Benfica's Gianluca Prestianni receives a provisional one-match Uefa suspension after Real Madrid's Vinicius Jr reported alleged racist abuse during last week's Champions League meeting.

item.title: The former kitman, 102, who left his mark on the World Cup
item.description: Charlie O'Leary was the Republic of Ireland kitman, but his influence extends to football across the island.

item.title: Police investigate 'unacceptable' online abuse of Rangers duo
item.description: Police Scotland is investigating after Rangers players Emmanuel Fernandez and Djeidi Gassama shared screenshots of racist abuse aimed at them on their social media accounts after Sunday's match in Livingston.

item.title: Loftus-Cheek out for around eight weeks with broken jaw
item.description: England midfielder Ruben Loftus-Cheek will be out for around eight weeks after having surgery on a broken jaw, AC Milan say.

item.title: Scotland lose Maclean and Cornet for World Cup qualifying openers
item.description: Scotland have lost midfield duo Kirsty Maclean and Chelsea Cornet to injury for their opening World Cup qualifiers against Luxembourg.

item.title: How Liverpool shook off 'horrendous' start to beat Forest
item.description: Match of the Day pundits Danny Murphy and Shay Given compare Liverpool's "flat" first-half performance against Nottingham Forest with their match-winning display in the second period.

item.title: Tudor 'certain' Spurs will stay up despite heavy defeat by Arsenal
item.description: Tottenham interim manager Igor Tudor says his side need "to work harder than before" but is confident of avoiding relegation, despite a 4-1 defeat by Arsenal in his first game.

item.title: Liverpool the 'lucky ones' in game of 'fine margins' - Slot
item.description: Liverpool head coach Arne Slot says his side were the "lucky ones" in a "game of fine margins" as a stoppage-time goal brings a 1-0 win at Nottingham Forest.

item.title: Win over Wolves 'big relief' for Palace - Glasner
item.description: Crystal Palace manager Oliver Glasner says he is relieved with his side's 1-0 win over Wolves and confirms his commitment to the club until the end of the season.

item.title: Defeat against Liverpool 'not fair' on Forest - Pereira
item.description: Vitor Pereira says "one point was a minimum" his Nottingham Forest side deserved against Liverpool and defeat "is not fair".

item.title: Celtic to appeal against Trusty red card decision
item.description: Celtic state their intention to appeal against the red card shown to Auston Trusty during Sunday's 2-1 home defeat by Hibernian.

item.title: Chelsea, Arsenal and Man City into Women's FA Cup quarters
item.description: Holders Chelsea edged out Manchester United to reach the quarter-finals of the Women's FA Cup, while Arsenal and Man City cruise through with comfortable wins.

item.title: How Sheff Wed's earliest-ever relegation unfolded
item.description: Sheffield Wednesday are the first EFL club to go down in February - here is how a season to forget unfolded.

item.title: How ruthless Arsenal exposed Tottenham's weaknesses
item.description: MOTD pundit Danny Murphy explains why, as well as Arsenal's superior quality in Sunday's north London derby, they exploited Tottenham's tactical weakness too.

item.title: Arsenal pass derby test of nerve as title race looks set to go to wire
item.description: Arsenal passed the north London derby test with flying colours as Premier League title race looks likely to go to the wire, says chief football writer Phil McNulty

item.title: Old Firm 'buckle under pressure' as incredible title race twists again
item.description: After a defeat for Celtic and surprise draw for Rangers, where does the latest weekend of drama leave the Scottish Premiership title race?

item.title: It's absolutely OK to face fans' criticism - Glasner
item.description: Oliver Glasner says it is "absolutely OK" to "face criticism" after some Crystal Palace fans again questioned his immediate future as manager.

item.title: 'We deserved a lot more' - Edwards on 'harsh' Crystal Palace defeat
item.description: Rob Edwards praises his side's performance and says "it feels harsh" for Wolves to be leaving with no points, after their 1-0 defeat to Crystal Palace at Selhurst Park in the Premier League.

item.title: 'Team spirit is still there' despite Fulham loss - Le Bris
item.description: Sunderland manager Regis Le Bris says it is not unusual for a newly promoted team to struggle but insists the Black Cats "team spirit is still there" despite losing 3-1 to Fulham.

item.title: Fulham were 'clinical' & showed 'maturity' - Silva
item.description: Fulham head coach Marco Silva says his side were "clinical" and showed "maturity" in their 3-1 win over Sunderland in the Premier League.

item.title: Chelsea's set-piece defending 'too easy' to play against - Richards
item.description: Match of the Day pundit Micah Richards criticises Chelsea's inability to show "leadership and organisation" in defence, highlighting how Burnley were able to easily attack the home side from set-pieces in their 1-1 draw at Stamford Bridge in the Premier League.

item.title: Chelsea lacked ruthlessness in Burnley draw - Rosenior
item.description: Chelsea head coach Liam Rosenior believes his side lacked the ruthlessness in their 1-1 draw with Burnley at Stamford Bridge in the Premier League.

item.title: 'Losing the dressing room' - what does it mean and how can it happen?
item.description: "Losing the dressing room" is now part of football's vocabulary. Chief football writer Phil McNulty asks what it means, how it happens and can it be solved?

item.title: 'Drive and hunger' take Milner to Premier League appearance record
item.description: Brighton's James Milner starts against Brentford to break the all-time Premier League appearance record in his 654th match.

item.title: The 20-year-old newspaper column that gave a hint to Man City's new tactics
item.description: Pep Guardiola is using new tactics this season by making Manchester City's attack more narrow, but did he hint at the idea 20 years ago?

item.title: England, Luton & Ted Lasso - Powell's 40 years in football
item.description: Luton Town assistant boss Chris Powell talks to the BBC about his 40 years in football.

item.title: Vinicius: Eight years at Real Madrid, 20 cases of alleged racist abuse
item.description: Spanish football expert Guillem Balague details how Vincius Jr has become a global symbol of resistance against discrimination.

item.title: 'We really messed up' - does European football need more balance?
item.description: One set of clubs dominate the Champions League, another win their domestic title every year but cannot compete - does European football have a problem?

item.title: All February's done deals, including deadline day, in one place
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: VAR vs TMO – what are the key differences?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks at the key differences between football's video assistant referees and rugby union's use of television match officials.

item.title: Who has the most Premier League appearances?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything Team look at which players have the highest total Premier League appearances.

item.title: Where do iconic football moves originate from?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team explain where the names of iconic football moves come from.

item.title: Why is there a four-week break in the WSL?
item.description: BBC Sport takes a look at why there is a second hiatus in the Women's Super League so soon after the winter break.

item.title: Why are some football shin pads so small?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team explore why some football shin pads are so small, and what the laws are around wearing them.

item.title: How does the Nations League work and when is the draw?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how the Uefa Nations League works ahead of the draw for the 2026-27 competition

item.title: What do you get for winning EFL Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains what the winner of the Carabao Cup gets.

item.title: 'Man Utd consider McTominay raid as clubs eye Robertson' - gossip
item.description: Manchester United are claimed to be considering re-signing Scott McTominay from Napoli, while three Premier League clubs are linked with Liverpool's Andy Robertson.

item.title: Forest 'enjoy' themselves for perfect start under Pereira
item.description: Vitor Pereira asks his Nottingham Forest players to "express themselves" prior to their commanding victory at Fenerbahce in the first leg of their Europa League knockout round play-off tie.

item.title: Watch live Bundesliga games on Friday nights on the BBC
item.description: BBC Sport has broadcasting rights for Bundesliga matches for the 2025-26 and 2026-27 seasons.

item.title: 'Golazo!'- why one of football's most iconic intros has returned
item.description: BBC Sport looks at a feature of Serie A coverage which is delighting fans once more, three decades after the height of its popularity.

item.title: Three years on, Deulofeu is still fighting for 'miracle' return
item.description: Gerard Deulofeu has not played for more than three years but believes he can make "history" by fighting for a "miracle" comeback.

item.title: Football must avoid 'microscopic' VAR - Uefa refs' chief
item.description: Uefa referees' chief Roberto Rosetti says video assistant review (VAR) is becoming too forensic and handball must be revisited.

item.title: Smith taken off on stretcher in Arsenal FA Cup win
item.description: Arsenal forward Olivia Smith is taken off on a stretcher during the Gunners' 3-0 victory over Bristol City in the Women's FA Cup.

item.title: Second only to England - why so many Swedes are in WSL
item.description: BBC World Service's More Than The Score podcast looks at the growing influence of Swedish talent into the Women's Super League.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.

item.title: Napoli 'smell blood' & target Premier League's discarded stars
item.description: After Scott McTominay was a hit, Napoli went back to the Premier League for Kevin de Bruyne, could more players follow them to Serie A?

item.title: When River Plate icon Diaz took over fourth-tier Oxford
item.description: Twenty years on from Ramon Diaz's surprising appointment at Oxford United, BBC Sport looks at what happened and how it went.
